title: Fire Animation Random Fact Site

requirements.text: Create a visually striking fire CSS/JS animation as the centerpiece of the page. The fire effect should use layered animated elements (flames, embers, glow) built with CSS keyframe animations and JavaScript-driven particle effects to simulate realistic, flickering fire.
acceptanceCriteria: A fire animation is prominently displayed on the page
The animation uses CSS keyframes for flame flicker and glow effects
JavaScript adds dynamic particle/ember elements to enhance realism
The animation runs continuously and smoothly on load

requirements.text: Add a 'Get a Random Fact' button styled to match the fire theme (e.g., glowing ember or molten look). When clicked, it displays a new random fact drawn from a built-in collection of at least 20 interesting facts. The fact text should appear with a smooth reveal animation.

quotes: gives you a random fact when u click a button

acceptanceCriteria: A clearly visible button is present on the page
Clicking the button displays a random fact from a hardcoded collection
Each click has a reasonable chance of showing a different fact
The fact appears with a smooth CSS transition or animation
At least 20 facts are included in the collection

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive fire-themed visual design across the entire page: dark background (near-black or deep charcoal), warm orange/red/amber color palette for text and UI elements, dramatic typography, and a layout that frames the fire animation and fact display card as focal points. Enforce the theme consistently across all components.
acceptanceCriteria: Page background is dark (near-black or deep charcoal)
Typography and accent colors use warm orange, red, and amber tones
The fact display area is styled as a card or panel with fire-themed styling
The overall design feels cohesive and dramatic
No off-theme colors (e.g., corporate blue) appear anywhere

requirements.text: Store the collection of random facts in the Motoko backend as a stable array. Expose a query method `getRandomFact()` that returns a random fact string. The frontend calls this method when the button is clicked.

quotes: gives you a random fact when u click a button

acceptanceCriteria: Backend has a stable array of at least 20 facts
The method returns a different fact pseudo-randomly on each call
Frontend successfully retrieves and displays facts from the backend

constraints: Fire animation must be implemented purely with CSS and JS — no external animation libraries (e.g., no GSAP, anime.js)
Facts must be served from the Motoko backend, not hardcoded only in the frontend
nonGoals: Ability for users to submit or save facts
